title: 3-Hydroxy-2-Methyl-1,4-Naphthoquinone Monoxime : A New Sensitive Reagent for the Determination of lron(II) and Cobalt(II)
author: (Miss) KAMINI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, University of Delhi, Delhi-110 007 

  Manuscript received 23 April 1981, revised 8 July 1982, accepted 28 January 1983  

 3-Hydroxy-2-methyl-1,4-naphthoquinone monoxime is suggested for the estimation of 21.5-114.0  µ g iron(Il) and 3.5-41.5  µ g cobalt(II) over the  p H range 4.8-7.0 and 5.0-7.5, respectively. The iron(ll) and cobalt(ll) complexes possess maximum absorption at 460 nm and 430 nm, respectively with the molar absorptivlties of 1.28 x 10 4  and 3.24 x 10 4  I.cm -1 . mole -1 . The determinations have been carried out in 50% ethanol. The limits of tolerance of foreign ions have been studied.
